questions.question: If p = (8 + 3√7) and q = (8 - 3√7), then what is the value of (p3 + q3)?
What is the reflection of point (-1, -5) on the line x - 3 = 0?
questions.explanation: Mid-point of original point and reflected point on the line is the foot of perpendicular on the line. The formula for finding the foot of perpendicular from point (x1, y1) on the line (ax + by + c = 0) is- (x - x1)/a = (y - y1)/b = -(ax1 + by1 + c)/(a2 + b2) (ax + by + c = 0) = (x - 3) and (x1, y1) = (-1, -5) After comparing- a = 1, b = 0, c = -3, x1 = -1 and y1 = -5 From the equation- (x + 1)/1 = (y + 5)/0 = -(1 * (-1) + 0 * (-5) - 3)/(12 + 02) (x + 1)/1 = (y + 5)/0 = 4 (x + 1)/1 = 4 x + 1 = 4 x = 3 (y + 5)/0 = 4 y + 5 = 0 y = -5 Co-ordinates of foot of perpendicular = (x, y) = (3, -5) Let co-ordinates of reflected point = (p, q) (p - 1)/2, (q - 5)/2 = (3, -5) (p - 1)/2 = 3 p = 7 (q - 5)/2 = -5 q = -5 Hence, co-ordinates of reflected point = (p, q) = (7, -5)

questions.question: What is the unit digit of 65th power of (2061 - 1986 + 678 - 259)?
questions.explanation: The concept of cyclicity is used to identify the last digit of the number. Cyclicity chart: NumberCyclicityUnit digit values Power 1Power 2Power 3Power 4 010 111 242486 343971 4246 515 616 747931 848426 9291 (Note: when remainder is 0, power = cyclicity) = (2061 - 1986 + 678 - 259) = 494 In 49465: Unit digit of 494 is 4. From the cyclicity chart, cyclicity of 4 is 2. So, we have to divide the power of 494 i.e.65 by 2. After dividing 65 by 2, remainder = 1. So, power ≠ cyclicity Unit digit of 41 = 4

questions.question: Aman's present age is 7 years more than Barun. After 6 years, Aman's age will be 35% greater than Barun's age at that time. What is the present age of Barun?
questions.explanation: Let the present age of Barun be 'x' years Present age of Aman = (x + 7) years So, (x + 7 + 6) = (135/100)*(x + 6) (x + 13) = (27/20)*(x + 6) x = 14

questions.question: What is the value of 'k' in the given equation? 3(k - 3) + √256 + √529 = 2(3 of 16 - 15 ÷ 3 + 8)
If A2 - B2 = 48, A + B + C = 18 and 2C = 3B, then what is the value of A: B: C? (A, B and C are positive integers)
questions.explanation: Given: A + B + C = 18 and 2C = 3B A + B = 18 - C B = 2C/3 Then, A = 18 - C - 2C/3 = 18 - 5C/3 Now, A2 - B2 = 48 Then, (A + B)(A - B) = 48 (18 - C)(18 - 5C/3 - 2C/3) = 48 C = 6, 19.71 (But C is an positive integer. So, C = 6) Then, B = 6 * 2/3 = 4 And, A = 18 - 6 * 5/3 = 8 Therefore, A: B: C = 8: 4: 6 = 4: 2: 3

questions.question: If 2 _ _ 7 6 is divisible by 6 and both the blanks in given number are filled by same digit, then which of the following value is not possible value for each blank?
questions.explanation: Let the value of each blank = a 2aa76 is divisible by 6 means the sum of all digits of given number is divisible by 3, i.e., = (2 + a + a + 7 + 6) is divisible by 3 = (15 + 2a) is divisible by 3 Here, 'a' should be a multiple of 3. Then, the possible values for 'a' = 3, 6 and 9

questions.question: One shirt is sold at Rs.450 and 5 packs of shirt (each pack contain 3 shirts) is sold at Rs.6345, then what is the effective discount percent given on the pack?
questions.explanation: Cost of one shirt = Rs.450 Cost of one shirt from the pack = 6345/(5 * 3) = Rs.423 Effective discount percent given on the pack = [(450 - 423)/450] * 100 = 6%

questions.question: A 1.5m tall man walks at a uniform speed of 60m/min away from a lamp post of height 6m. Find the rate at which his shadow increases?
questions.explanation: Let AB be the post and CD be the man. Let y be his distance from the pole and x be his shadow. Wherever he goes the ratio x:y will be constant. (x+y):x = 6:1.5 = 4 => y = 3x or x = y/3 Hence his shadow will move at 1/3rd of his speed. Therefore speed of shadow = 20 m/min

questions.question: A circular well of diameter 1.2 m was dug in a circular land of diameter 2.6 m. The mud that was removed by digging the well was evenly spread on the rest of the land. If the level of the ground was raised by 3m, find the volume of the mud taken out while digging the well.
questions.explanation: Area of the land filled with mud = area of field - area of well = π(R2 - r2) = 22/7 x (1.69 - 0.36) = 22/7 x 1.33 = 4.18 sq m Volume of mud = area filled with land x raise in level = 4.18 x 3 = 12.54 m3

questions.question: Mr Biswas bought a new apartment making a cash down payment of Rs. 2028000 and agreed to pay two more yearly installments of equivalent amount in next two years. If the rate of interest is 4% per annum, compounded annually, what is the price of the apartment?
questions.explanation: Let Mr Biswas paid P1 for the first year and P2 for two years as principal values(without interest) Now, 2028000=P1(1+4/100) => P1=1950000 2028000=P2(1+4/100)2 => P2=1875000 The price of the apartment= 2028000+1950000+1875000=5853000

questions.question: In a triangle ∆ ABC, ∠B is 40% more than ∠A and ∠C is 40% less than ∠A. Find the value of exterior angle corresponding to the largest angle.
questions.explanation: => ∠B=∠A[1+(40/100)] => ∠B= (7/5)∠A => ∠C=∠A[1-(40/100)] => ∠B= (3/5)∠A => ∠A + (7/5)∠A +(3/5)∠A =180 => 3∠A=180 => ∠A=60 => ∠B=84 => ∠C=36 => Largest angle is ∠B=84 exterior angle corresponding to ∠B=180-84=96 Correct answer is option a

questions.question: In a skating school, there were 60 skaters who used to do skating. If 20 more skaters joined the school, then the monthly income of skating teacher increases by Rs 3000 per month, while the average fees per skater reduces by Rs 25. Find the original fees of skater?
questions.explanation: Let the original fees of each skater be Rs x. Then total fees of 60 skaters = 60x. Given, 60x + 3000 = 80(x-25) So, 20x = 5000 Therefore, original fees of the skater = Rs 250

questions.question: Out of total number of students from 8th, 9th and 10th standards 75%, 90% and 80% students passed respectively. If ratio of total number students in each of 8th , 9th and 10th standard are 4:5:6 respectively and total number of students passed from these 3 standards is 123, then how many students are passed from 9th standard?
questions.explanation: Let total number of students in 8th ,9th and 10th standard are 4S,5S and 6S respectively. Total number of students passed from these 3 standards = (0.75 x4S) + (0.9 x 5S) + (0.8 x 6S) = 3S +4.5S +4.8S 12.3S = 123 S = 10 Total number of students in 9th standard = 5 x 10 = 50 Number of students passed from 9th standard = 0.9 x 50 = 45.

questions.question: An amount of money is to be divided among P, Q and R in the ratio of 3 : 5 : 7 respectively. If the amount received by R is Rs. 4,000 more than the amount received by Q, what will be the total amount received by P and Q together?
questions.explanation: Let the amount received by P, Q and R be 3x, 5x and 7x respectively Total money received = 3x + 5x + 7x = 15x Also, it is given that 7x = 4000 + 5x 2x = 4000 X = 2000 Therefore, total money = 15 X 2000 = Rest 30000 Total amount received by P and Q = 8x = 8(2000) = Rest 16000 Option c is correct

questions.question: sin(2x - 20°) = cos(2y + 20°), find sin(x+y) given that x and y are acute angles and x+y < 90°

A can complete a project in 20 days and B can complete the same project in 30 days. A and B start working on the project together and A quits 10 days before the project is completed. In how many days is the project actually completed?
questions.explanation: Let the project involve 60 units of work. (Since, 60 is the LCM of 20 and 30) Hence, A can do 60/20 = 3 units of work per day and B can do 60/30 = 2 units of work per day. After A quits, B is working alone on the project for the last 10 days. ∴ Work done by B in last 10 days = 2(10) = 20 units. ∴ Work done by A and B together = 60 − 20 = 40 units A and B can together do 3 + 2 = 5 units per day ∴ Time for which A and B worked together = 40/5 = 8 days ∴ Total time in which project is actually completed = 8 + 10 = 18 days Hence, option c is correct.

questions.question: After travelling for 3 hour and 15 minutes at a speed of 40 km/hr Vishnu realized that he has covered only 5/8 of the total distance. If he has to cover the entire distance in 4.5 hours then at what speed he should travel for the remaining time period?
questions.explanation: Total distance covered by Vishnu in 3 hour 15 minutes = (40 X 3.25) km = 130 km Now total distance that Vishnu has to cover = (130 X 8/5) km = 208 km Now remaining distance that Vishnu has to travel = (208 - 130) km = 78 Km Now the speed of Vishnu for the remaining period = (78/1.25) km/hr = 62.4 km/hr

questions.question: Ram, Sham and Mohan start a business with investments in the ratio 1:2:3. They make a profit of 6 lakhs after one year. If Mohan and Sham leave after 4 and 8 months respectively, then what is Ram's share of total profit?
questions.explanation: It is given that Ram, Sham and Mohan start a business with investments in the ratio 1:2:3 Initial contribution of Ram Rs "a" Initial contribution of Sham is Rs 2a And initial contribution of Mohan is 3a It is given that Mohan and Sham leave the business after 4 and 8 months respectively. Total contribution of Ram, Mohan and Sham over twelve month period is: Contribution of Ram = (Initial investment x Period of investment) = a x 12 = 12a Contribution of Sham = 2a x 8 = 16a Contribution of Mohan = 3a x 4 = 12 a Share of Ram in total profit = Contribution of Ram/ Total contribution = 12a/(12a + 16a + 12a) = 12/40 = 3/10 But it is given that total profit = Rs 600000 Therefore, Ram's profit = (3/10) x (600000) = Rs 180000

questions.question: Find the total marks obtained by Sreyashi and Soham together.
What will me Debarghya's total marks if all the full marks are converted to 100?
Find the average marks of all the students in the subject Mathematics.
questions.explanation: The required average marks=(141+132+126+114+117+135)/6 =127.5

questions.question: Find the percentage of marks obtained by Sayak.
The number obtained by interchanging the digits of a two digit number is lesser than the original number by 54. If the sum of the two digit of the number is 12, then what is the original number.
questions.explanation: Suppose required number = (10x + y) Where x > y According to the question, (10x + y) - (10y + x) = 54 9x -9y = 54 9 (x - y) = 54 (x - y) = 6 ........(i) And we know that, (x + y) = 12 ...........(ii) From eq. (i) & (ii), x - y = 6 x + y =12 2x = 18 x = 9 Putting that value in eq. (ii), 9 + y = 12 y =3 Number = 10*9+3 = 93
